sample_worlds._note: Design proposals only. No runtime implementation exists. Each entry references the backing promoted artifacts.

worlds.title: Dual Reality View
worlds.visitor_question: What does a wormhole look like, and how do we know the bending is real?

worlds.title: Observer Disagreement
worlds.visitor_question: How much does curved transport change what you see vs. straight?

worlds.title: Hermetic Closure
worlds.visitor_question: When does the integrator fail silently, and what does failure look like?

worlds.title: Cathedral Probe
worlds.visitor_question: How do you diagnose a renderer that produces wrong pixels without crashing?

worlds.title: Transport Coherence Basin
worlds.visitor_question: Where in the scene does transport refuse to converge, and why there?

artifacts.title: Wormhole Dual Reality — Six Steps from Perception to Interpretation
artifacts.summary: Six frames that walk a visitor from bare curved render → straight-path comparison → curvature heat map → semantic glyphs → collision radar → full interpretive stack. The clearest public demonstration of DualRealityTransport.
artifacts.next_step: Animate as a crossfade carousel. Add a seventh frame showing the derivative step count heatmap.

artifacts.title: Wormhole Structure Observatory — Six Diagnostic Views of One Scene
artifacts.summary: The same wormhole rendered six ways: clean curved, straight vs. curved, depth heatmap, step budget heatmap, domain diagnostics, structure minimap. Plus a resonance chamber overlay synthesizing all diagnostic layers into one annotated image.
artifacts.next_step: Run the observatory on additional scene types (GRIN room, mirror portal) for comparison.

artifacts.title: Atomic Orbital GRIN Observatory — Light Bent by a Hydrogen Field
artifacts.summary: Five renders spanning no-field baseline to exaggerated hydrogen GRIN lensing to temporally modulated orbital fringes. Demonstrates GRIN field rendering at the physics level: 2–11% of pixels visibly shift between temporal ticks.
artifacts.next_step: Add helium (two-electron) variant. Render at higher resolution for finer fringe detail.

artifacts.title: Overspace First Milestone — Approaching the Gallery Orb
artifacts.summary: Five path-progress frames (0–80%) from an auto-path camera approaching a gallery orb through overspace. The first complete render of a non-Euclidean scene in xPRIMEray. Proves the auto-path, GRIN integrator, and overlay pipeline work together under a moving observer.
artifacts.next_step: Render the 100% approach frame (orb entry). Animate the sequence as the site hero.

artifacts.title: Observer Disagreement — How Curved Transport Changes What You See
artifacts.summary: 23.8% of pixels classify differently between curved GRIN and straight reference transport. The dominant shift (27k pixels): geometry hits become escaped rays — GRIN deflects them past surfaces entirely. Quantifies what curvature costs the observer in missed surface contacts.
artifacts.next_step: Build a disagreement heatmap over observer pose space. Map the angular dependence.

artifacts.title: Hermetic Hit Closure — The Budget Required for Complete Integration
artifacts.summary: Budget=32: 0% closure. Budget=700: 100% closure. A cliff edge separates plausible-looking renders from correctly classified ones. Failure storyboard shows the spatial clustering of failures; recovery heatmap shows where adaptive scaling rescues them.
artifacts.next_step: Map the closure cliff surface across (step_size, budget) space.

artifacts.title: DOE Scheduler Resonance — Why Stride 4 Destroys the Image
artifacts.summary: Stride 2: 26% band coverage. Stride 4: 0.3% coverage. A 68-cell DOE reveals systematic resonance between row-skip cadence and scene curvature geometry. The heatmap makes the periodic collapse structure visible. Motivated the tile scheduler that solved it.
artifacts.next_step: Repeat sweep with tile scheduler to confirm resonance elimination. Publish as canonical sampling-resonance illustration.

artifacts.title: Transport Coherence Basin — 289 Instability Regions
artifacts.summary: 289 UNSEALED_NONCONVERGENT regions, all requiring precision=0.003125, all clustered symmetrically around the GRIN field boundary annulus. Uniformity of the precision floor is itself a finding — it points to a topological feature rather than random noise.
artifacts.next_step: Re-run after softening the IOR gradient at the GRIN outer shell to test whether the instability is parameterization-dependent.

artifacts.title: DOE Overnight — The Resolution Cost of Seeing the Curvature Band
artifacts.summary: Step=0.00625 captures 26% of the curvature band. Step=0.025 captures 0.6%. An 18-cell overnight sweep establishes the step-length operating floor and documents that telemetry-on changes pixel output — confirming it must be treated as a separate rendering mode.
artifacts.next_step: Run adaptive step length to test whether fine-step quality is achievable at coarse-step cost.

artifacts.title: Recursive Mirror Ghost Portal — Phase 1: Material System
artifacts.summary: Phase 1 complete: PerfectMirrorReflection and DielectricRefraction validated at 140k crossing events/frame with zero stalls. Dedicated scene not yet built; benchmark PNG pending. Intended to become the project's front-page hero image.
artifacts.next_step: Create test-recursive-mirror-ghost-portal.tscn. Run testbench. Promote the resulting PNG.
